core_mechanism: a semiclassical phase-space (truncated-Wigner) sampling scheme in which intrinsic quantum fluctuations are encoded as random initial conditions drawn from a phase-space quasi-probability distribution, propagated through the deterministic nonlinear field equations, and ensemble-averaged to approximate the fluctuating quantum dynamics
brief_reason: The abstract frames the entire method as numerically simulating the system 'using the classical field method via the truncated Wigner approximation,' i.e., replacing quantum-field evolution with an ensemble of stochastically-initialized classical field trajectories.
